reasoning: The effective yield has been on a sharp upward trajectory in March, driven by stickier inflation data, shifting Fed expectations (with markets now considering late-2026 rate hikes), and weak Treasury auction results. The FRED trend analysis gives a combined probability of 0.53. While a potential Iran ceasefire introduced some downward pressure on yields around March 25 due to falling energy prices, the dominant macroeconomic factors remain heavily skewed toward sustained higher yields. Therefore, it is slightly more likely than not that the yield will continue its broad upward drift by April 5.
